iris quickstart — the guided first session

This is the plain guide: the tour's steps as numbered copy-paste commands,
executing nothing. Run `iris quickstart` in an interactive terminal for the
guided version: two acts, one question opening each, the steps then running
for real — writing the embedded hello_iris sample (pipelines/hello_iris/
and schemas/demo/colors/) into the workspace for you.

The engine — provision it and start it:

  1. Bootstrap the engine: place the managed Postgres, create the meta and data databases, set up the control socket.
     iris engine install

  2. Start the engine daemon in the background; it stays running after the tour.
     iris engine start -d

  3. Read the engine readout: versions, socket, Postgres mode, role, uptime.
     iris engine info

The pipeline — register, run, ask:

  4. Register the hello_iris sample: its pipeline, role, grants, and the demo.colors table.
     iris declare apply pipelines/hello_iris

  5. Run it: the script upserts the seven rainbow colors into demo.colors; re-running layers a second provenance stamp on the same rows.
     iris pipeline run hello_iris

  6. Ask a row who wrote it: the authoring run, the layer stack, the hashes.
     iris data provenance demo.colors green

Every step is idempotent and safe to re-run; stop the engine later with
`iris engine stop`.
